source_docs.title: Full-length mRNA-Seq from single-cell levels of RNA and individual circulating tumor cells

oligos.name: SMART CDS Primer II A (tailed oligo-dT RT primer)
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription priming oligo; anneals to the mRNA poly(A) tail and seeds first-strand cDNA synthesis

components.name: Poly(dT)30VN
components.role: poly(dT) with VN anchor that positions priming at the 5' end of the poly(A) tail (V = A/C/G)

oligos.notes: Reported as 5'-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTACT(30)VN-3'; V represents A, C or G. Keep IUPAC letters V and N literal.

oligos.name: SMARTer II A oligonucleotide (Template Switching Oligo, TSO)
oligos.role: Template-switching oligo; its terminal riboG's base-pair with the nontemplated C's added by RT, extending the template so the RT appends a universal anchor to the 3' end of the first-strand cDNA

components.name: Riboguanosines
components.role: 3' rGrGrG that anneal to the CCC overhang on the cDNA (template switching)

oligos.notes: Reported as 5'-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTACATrGrGrG-3'; rGrGrG folded to GGG.

oligos.name: IS PCR primer (cDNA amplification primer)
oligos.role: Single primer used for PCR pre-amplification of full-length cDNA; matches the IS anchor present at both ends of the template-switched cDNA
oligos.notes: Reported as 5'-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT-3'. A single primer amplifies both ends because the CDS primer and TSO share this sequence.

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: Flow-cell grafting sequence at the 5' end of the final library (PE/TruSeq path)
oligos.notes: Not printed in the documents; standard Illumina P5 for the 'Illumina PE adaptors' referenced by the PE protocol.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 1 primer
oligos.role: Read 1 sequencing primer / 5' adapter of the final library (PE path)
oligos.notes: Standard TruSeq Read 1 sequence for the Illumina PE adaptors used in the 'PE' library protocol; exact sequence not printed in the documents.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 2 primer
oligos.role: Read 2 sequencing primer / 3' adapter of the final library (PE path)
oligos.notes: Standard TruSeq Read 2 sequence for the Illumina PE adaptors; exact sequence not printed in the documents.

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: Flow-cell grafting sequence at the 3' end of the final library (appears as its reverse complement on the top strand)
oligos.notes: Not printed in the documents; standard Illumina P7. Top-strand 3' end is the reverse complement: ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG.

oligos.name: Illumina PE (TruSeq) adapter
oligos.role: Y-shaped double-stranded adapter ligated to end-repaired, A-tailed cDNA fragments in the PE library protocol

components.name: Partial TruSeq Read 1 / P5 branch
components.role: P5-proximal branch of the forked adapter

oligos.notes: The 'Illumina PE adaptors' named in the PE protocol; exact adapter oligos not printed in the documents. Sequences shown are the standard TruSeq PE adapter constants.

oligos.name: Nextera Tn5 transposon adapter (Mosaic End)
oligos.role: Adapter loaded on the Tn5 transposase in the alternative 'Tn5' tagmentation library protocol; simultaneously fragments cDNA and adds Nextera sequences
oligos.notes: The Nextera/Tn5 tagmentation path is described as an alternative to PE. Only the conserved 19-bp Mosaic End is given; full Nextera i5/i7 adapters are not printed in the documents.

final_library.source_label: Smart-Seq final Illumina library (PE / TruSeq path)

evidence.locator: Smart-Seq final Illumina library (PE / TruSeq path)

library_sequencing.note: Primer is identical to the top strand, anneals to the bottom strand and reads rightward into one end of the cDNA insert. No cell barcode or UMI, so Read 1 is pure cDNA; read length varied by run (Supplementary Table 1).

Primer is identical to the top-strand Read 2 adapter, anneals to the bottom strand and reads rightward across the i7 sample index (single index in the original TruSeq PE chemistry). The output is the top-strand i7 sequence.

Primer anneals to the top-strand Read 2 adapter (its reverse complement) and reads leftward into the opposite end of the cDNA insert (paired-end), again pure cDNA. Shown below aligned 3'->5' under its binding site.

title: Smart-Seq
description: Smart-Seq is a plate-based, full-length single-cell (or low-input, down to ~10 pg) mRNA-Seq method. Poly(A)+ RNA is captured by a tailed oligo(dT) CDS primer and reverse-transcribed by MMLV/SMARTScribe RT, which adds a few nontemplated C's at the 5' end of the mRNA; a SMARTer II A template-switching oligo then base-pairs with these C's so the RT copies the oligo, appending a universal anchor. The resulting full-length cDNA carries the same IS PCR sequence at both ends and is PCR pre-amplified (12–18 cycles), then converted to a standard Illumina library either by Covaris shearing + TruSeq (PE) adapter ligation or by Nextera Tn5 tagmentation. Because there is no cell barcode or UMI, each cell is one indexed library and the paired reads tile the full length of the cDNA insert, enabling isoform, allele and SNP analysis.

reference.label: Full-length mRNA-Seq from single-cell levels of RNA and individual circulating tumor cells

throughput.summary: Plate/tube-based, one indexed library per cell; the study made Smart-Seq libraries from 42 individual human or mouse cells plus 64 total-RNA dilution libraries, each sequenced to typically >20 million uniquely mapping reads.
throughput.cells: 42 individual cells (single-cell resolution; ~8,000 genes detected per cell)
throughput.rna: Applicable from 10 ng down to 10 pg total RNA (single-cell level)

publication.statistical_model: Single-cell gene expression treated as log-normally distributed (RPKM, log2); differential expression tested by one-way ANOVA with Tukey post-hoc and Benjamini-Hochberg FDR (no UMI count model, as reads are full-length and non-UMI).

other.label: Commercial kit
other.value: Clontech SMARTer Ultra Low RNA Kit for Illumina sequencing

other.label: Library prep options
other.value: Covaris shearing + Illumina PE (TruSeq) ligation, or Nextera Tn5 tagmentation

data_processing.summary: Reads from each single-cell (one indexed library per cell) are aligned to both genome and transcriptome, resolved to uniquely mapping positions, and quantified as per-gene RPKM; because reads are full-length and carry no UMI, there is no molecular collapsing. Downstream analyses cover differential expression, alternative-splicing (exon inclusion) and SNP/variant calling.
data_processing.statistical_model: Log-normal gene expression (RPKM, log2); differential expression by one-way ANOVA with Tukey post-hoc and Benjamini-Hochberg FDR; no UMI/count collapsing because reads are full-length and non-UMI.
